page: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the n4a server family. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Operating with a compute-focused resource allocation, the instance provides dedicated CPU resources without shared-core burstable limitations. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions this server as a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring dedicated processing power without the overhead of excess memory capacity.

This hardware profile is suited for lightweight compute-intensive tasks, microservices, small-scale web serving, and basic batch processing pipelines. The absence of local storage and GPUs makes it appropriate for stateless application tiers where persistent data is offloaded to external storage or database services.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine within the n4a server family, configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 4.0 GB of system memory. This configuration establishes a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, allocating 2.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Designed for compute-focused workloads, the dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without the resource contention typical of shared-core environments. This resource density makes the instance a cost-efficient choice for stateless microservices, lightweight batch processing, and small-scale web serving where memory requirements are minimal and external storage solutions are utilized.

tagline: A dedicated compute instance featuring a balanced two-to-one vCPU-to-memory ratio for stateless workloads and lightweight processing tasks.
